# Catalog

What each skill is and what it's grounded in тАФ per-skill provenance lives in each `skill.json`. To route by task, use [Pick a skill](./README.md#pick-a-skill); to install, see [Install](./README.md#install).

## Heuristic audit & design pairs

Source-grounded heuristics for software surfaces, split so the name says what it does. A **`-audit`** skill audits, debugs, or risk-scans an *existing* surface (expert lenses тЖТ severity-scored findings report, with an optional tracking ledger); a **`-design`** skill shapes a *new* one (names the good-shaped pattern тЖТ design doc / plan / runbook). A domain's pair shares its playbooks, lenses, personas, and rubrics via `skills/_shared/<domain>/`, so the two stay in lockstep without drift.

Grounded in 120+ cited sources тАФ Norman, Nielsen, Bloch, Gregg, the Google SRE book, Kleppmann, WCAG 2.2, Ousterhout, Parnas, and more. `minimal-modular-code` is the family's one singleton: a single skill spanning both review and design for code minimality and parallel-readiness.

- **`artifact-host-integration`** тАФ Implement the contract between a portable HTML artifact and its editing host тАФ postMessage tweak-panel handshake, on-disk persistence markers, fixed-canvas scaling, speaker-notes sync, comment/scene anchors, direct-edit-ready markup, and standalone bundling/export.
- **`docs-audit`** *(audit)* тАФ Audit a docs/help/agent-readable surface for friction, drift, accessibility, retrieval, or audience conflict and score it тАФ or debug a concrete docs failure.
- **`docs-design`** *(design)* тАФ Plan or reshape a docs surface before implementation тАФ information architecture, mode taxonomy, README/quickstart/reference structure, examples strategy, and API/tool-contract docs.
- **`dx-audit`** *(audit)* тАФ Audit a developer-experience surface (APIs, SDKs, CLIs, errors, setup, auth, packaging, IDE, plugins, telemetry) for friction and score it, or run a pre-ship edge-case risk pass.
- **`dx-design`** *(design)* тАФ Design a new developer-experience surface from scratch тАФ API, SDK, CLI, error envelope, setup/first-run flow, auth model, migration/deprecation path, plugin contract, or package scheme.
- **`minimal-modular-code`** тАФ Keep code minimal and right-sized for AI coding agents: DO (keep a change minimal тАФ reuse, subtract, avoid the wrong abstraction, check blast radius), REVIEW (audit existing code or a repo for slop and parallel-readiness, then score it), DESIGN (shape right-sized module boundaries and work-partitioning, sequence a safe refactor, or explain a principle). Replaces the clean-architecture audit/design pair under a minimalism thesis.
- **`test-audit`** *(audit)* тАФ Review a test suite for smells, redundancy, false-pass risk, brittleness, and flakiness and score it, or triage one failing, flaky, or slow test.
- **`test-design`** *(design)* тАФ Author a new test or test plan, shape a cross-layer test strategy (what to test at which layer), or plan which low-value tests to delete.
- **`ui-design`** *(design)* тАФ Produce or polish user-facing visual UI тАФ product screens and dashboards, design systems with tokens, interactive prototypes, motion, slide decks, and artifact handoff.
- **`ux-audit`** *(audit)* тАФ Audit an end-user product UX or accessibility surface тАФ usability flows, form friction, navigation/IA, error/recovery copy, and WCAG/keyboard checks тАФ and score it.
- **`writing-audit`** *(audit)* тАФ Audit an existing piece of writing тАФ revise wordy prose, copyedit mechanics while preserving voice, or diagnose why a draft buries the point or fails to land тАФ routed by intent (revise/copyedit/diagnose) ├Ч genre, emitting a scored findings report.
- **`writing-design`** *(design)* тАФ Structure, draft, or add a persuasive arc to a new piece of writing тАФ argument/memo, technical doc, talk/pitch, narrative, or general prose тАФ routed by intent (structure/draft/persuade) ├Ч genre.

## research

- **`research`** тАФ Source-grounded research in **two decision-frames**. `report` тАФ open-ended research on a topic, with citations and no decision attached (primer, literature review, state-of-the-art; depth modes `brief` / `survey` / `deep-dive`). `opportunity` тАФ validate a named product/business/market/feature opportunity across 14 areas (market, customer, competitive, domain, technical, data, operational, financial, legal, channel, GTM, stakeholder, risk, trend), ending in an F/A/D/R go/no-go/pivot decision with sub-agent fan-out per area. Every load-bearing claim is cited or marked as inference (report frame); every branch ends in a decision (opportunity frame).

## Agent experience (AX)

The agent-mirror family. An **AI agent is an actor**, not a peer audience тАФ so each human-experience domain gets an agent mirror: `agent-dx` (developer), `agent-docs` (reader), `agent-ux` (end-user), `agent-ops` (operator, and the family's front door), `agent-test` (subject under measurement). Two standalone arms тАФ `harden-repo-for-coding-agents` and `rules-from-coding-agent-failures` тАФ handle repo hardening and rule promotion, with `agent-ops` routing out to them. Each mirror does do / review / design.

- **`harden-repo-for-coding-agents`** тАФ Assess, harden, scaffold, and diagnose a repository's agent-readiness for AI coding harnesses (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Copilot, Windsurf, Aider). Harness-agnostic and portable-first; no eval/telemetry prerequisite тАФ it scaffolds from project knowledge (stack, layout, commands, invariants). Start here for first-pass scaffolding.
- **`rules-from-coding-agent-failures`** тАФ Capture observed agent failures in a per-file reflection log and promote recurring patterns into AGENTS.md rules / hooks / CI gates via the W1 тЙе3-entry floor. For teams with a feedback signal тАФ eval suites, run-level telemetry, A/B baselines, or a skill catalog under test.
- **`agent-docs`** тАФ Agent-as-reader: the agent-native docs an AI agent reads and acts on тАФ AGENTS.md, llms.txt, tool descriptions, machine-readable reference, context budget. Write or fix one, audit an existing one for findability and trigger clarity, or design the contract from scratch. Human and dual-audience docs stay in `docs-audit` / `docs-design`.
- **`agent-dx`** тАФ Agent-as-developer: keep the SDK / tool / error / telemetry surface an AI agent consumes minimal and safe. Keep an agent-facing change consumable, audit a surface for stable contracts and safe recovery, or design a new SDK / tool / MCP / structured-output surface. The agent layer above the human HTTP-client floor in `dx-audit` / `dx-design`.
- **`agent-ops`** тАФ Agent-as-operator, and the front door to the agent family. Operate a running agent system: observability, trace-and-eval loops, autonomy, budgets, gates. Wire them up, audit them and score maturity, or design the loop and route work to the right sibling skill. Start here when you're not sure which agent skill you need.
- **`agent-test`** тАФ Agent-as-subject: design the measurement an AI agent or skill is judged by тАФ evals, LLM-as-judge, trajectory tests, benchmarks, activation tests. Write the smallest gating eval, audit an existing suite or judge for trustworthiness, or design a failure-mode-first suite or held-out benchmark.
- **`agent-ux`** тАФ Agent-as-end-user: the surface an AI agent acts through (a UI, app, or computer-use target), often for a human. Make it perceivable and safe to act on, audit it for machine-readable state and approval gates, or design the state, action handles, and human/agent dual path. The agent-actor analog of human UX.

## Cross-agent interop & tooling

Skills that drive *another* coding agent or CLI inside your own workflow тАФ a second-opinion reviewer, an external analysis pass, a cross-project reflection тАФ instead of auditing or designing a surface themselves. The pragmatic "tools I actually reach for" corner of the kit.

- **`claude-code-cli`** тАФ Drive the external **Claude Code CLI** (`claude -p`) as an independent second-opinion reviewer and analysis agent тАФ review working-tree/staged/branch diffs (the wrapper builds the diff and feeds it read-only via `--permission-mode plan`), get a second opinion on a decision/bug/plan, run cross-project reflection, or hand off to a hosted `claude ultrareview`. Routes through a use-case registry, defaults to read-only, never bypasses permissions without an explicit ask, and presents output as an external opinion to reconcile against local evidence.
- **`codex-cli`** тАФ Drive the external **Codex CLI** as an independent second-opinion reviewer and analysis agent тАФ `codex review` for uncommitted/branch/commit diffs, `codex exec` (read-only sandbox) for second opinions and repo analysis, plus cross-project reflection, setup diagnostics, and prompt-prep (`--dry-run`) modes. Routes through a use-case registry, defaults to read-only, and never bypasses Codex approvals/sandbox without an explicit ask. Reach for it when you want a different LLM provider's take on code or docs before shipping.
- **`cursor-cli`** тАФ Drive the external **Cursor CLI** (`cursor-agent -p`) as an independent second-opinion reviewer and analysis agent тАФ review working-tree/staged/branch diffs (the wrapper builds the diff and feeds it read-only via `--mode plan`) or get a second opinion on a decision/bug/plan. Its edge over the other interop skills is **model diversity**: cursor-agent can run many providers' models (gpt-5, sonnet-4, тАж), so you can review code with a *different* model than wrote it. Routes through a use-case registry, defaults to read-only, never drops the guard with `--force`/`--yolo` without an explicit ask.

## Context budget & agent-setup hygiene

Audit and reclaim the per-session context your agent's *local setup* costs тАФ the MCP servers, plugins, skills, slash commands, and subagents that load into every session whether or not you use them. This family measures what's loaded, estimates its always-on token cost from real usage, and gates safe pruning. One skill today.

- **`context-budget-audit`** тАФ Audit the per-session **context/token cost** of your local agent setup тАФ idle MCP servers, unused plugins, skills, slash commands, and subagents. A read-only Python engine inventories each surface, estimates its always-on token cost, checks recent transcripts for real usage, and ranks what's safe to prune тАФ idle MCP servers are usually the biggest win. Removal is gated, never automatic.
